cells.text: 1f extra recovery on whiff; releasing the button on frames 7-17 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; great range and frame advantage for follow-up combos and pressure; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

cells.text: Good poke and Drive Rush starter
cells.html: Good poke and Drive Rush starter

cells.text: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; solid poke with good hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts opponent into free juggle state; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles
cells.html: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; solid poke with good hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts opponent into free juggle state; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

cells.text: Releasing the button on frames 5-13 results in uncharged version with 10f startup; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes free juggle state
cells.html: Releasing the button on frames 5-13 results in uncharged version with 10f startup; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes free juggle state

cells.text: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Marisa's only 4f normal; special cancel is delayed until after active frames; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame
cells.html: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Marisa's only 4f normal; special cancel is delayed until after active frames; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

cells.text: Long range for a cancelable 2MP (but slower than average startup); topmost hurtboxes on frames 8-15 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)
cells.html: Long range for a cancelable 2MP (but slower than average startup); topmost hurtboxes on frames 8-15 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

cells.text: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; slow but solid anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-5 only hit airborne opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (higher launch vs. airborne, and much higher on Punish Counter)
cells.html: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; slow but solid anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-5 only hit airborne opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (higher launch vs. airborne, and much higher on Punish Counter)

cells.text: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms
cells.html: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms

cells.text: 1f extra recovery on whiff/block; solid low poke with good range and hitbox priority; strong Drive Rush starter that links into 4HP or 5MP~MP at close range, 2MP at mid range, or 6MP from farther out; topmost hurtboxes on frames 9-16 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 17~)
cells.html: 1f extra recovery on whiff/block; solid low poke with good range and hitbox priority; strong Drive Rush starter that links into 4HP or 5MP~MP at close range, 2MP at mid range, or 6MP from farther out; topmost hurtboxes on frames 9-16 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 17~)

cells.text: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; has juggle potential; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)
cells.html: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; has juggle potential; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

cells.text: 3f extra recovery on whiff; has juggle potential and keeps the opponent in a limited juggle state (e.g. corner OTG bounce > 2[HK] > SA2); releasing the button on frames 7-15 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 8~)
cells.html: 3f extra recovery on whiff; has juggle potential and keeps the opponent in a limited juggle state (e.g. corner OTG bounce > 2[HK] > SA2); releasing the button on frames 7-15 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 8~)

cells.text: Strong forward movement and can link into light normals after Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush; has 2 hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-ups; useful link option out of DR~2MK due to its range
cells.html: Strong forward movement and can link into light normals after Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush; has 2 hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-ups; useful link option out of DR~2MK due to its range

cells.text: 3f extra recovery on whiff; can hold button 17f for improved properties; causes air reset when juggled into without Drive Rush; short range but important in combos and pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame
cells.html: 3f extra recovery on whiff; can hold button 17f for improved properties; causes air reset when juggled into without Drive Rush; short range but important in combos and pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

cells.text: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state
cells.html: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

cells.text: Can hold button 17f for improved propertied; has a Target Combo follow-up; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents
cells.html: Can hold button 17f for improved propertied; has a Target Combo follow-up; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

cells.html: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit

cells.text: Requires Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush 5LP to combo; 3f gap on block between hits that allows for a frame trap
cells.html: Requires Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush 5LP to combo; 3f gap on block between hits that allows for a frame trap

cells.text: Great pressure and bully tool from 5MP; true blockstring between hits; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame
cells.html: Great pressure and bully tool from 5MP; true blockstring between hits; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

cells.text: () refers to scaled damage after 5HP combo starter; 6f gap between hits on block (1f gap if starting from 5[HP]); grants a safe jump near the corner on a grounded hit or low-height juggle
cells.html: () refers to scaled damage after 5HP combo starter; 6f gap between hits on block (1f gap if starting from 5[HP]); grants a safe jump near the corner on a grounded hit or low-height juggle

cells.text: Low follow-up; true blockstring from 6MP, so an opponent mashing a crouching button after blocked 6MP will not be frame trapped; useful confirm if 6MP hits crouching opponent
cells.html: Low follow-up; true blockstring from 6MP, so an opponent mashing a crouching button after blocked 6MP will not be frame trapped; useful confirm if 6MP hits crouching opponent

cells.text: Whiffs on crouching opponents; if the opponent is standing, this ender is preferable for its corner carry and Super cancelability; Super cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame
cells.html: Whiffs on crouching opponents; if the opponent is standing, this ender is preferable for its corner carry and Super cancelability; Super cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

cells.text: Gives a risky 2nd overhead after 3HP; true blockstring, so opponent can safely mash a standing normal after blocking 3HP without being frame trapped or hit by the overhead; can be juggled into (KD +31); can whiff at longer ranges of 3HP (much more unsafe)
cells.html: Gives a risky 2nd overhead after 3HP; true blockstring, so opponent can safely mash a standing normal after blocking 3HP without being frame trapped or hit by the overhead; can be juggled into (KD +31); can whiff at longer ranges of 3HP (much more unsafe)

cells.text: Hitconfirmable ender after 6HK; delaying the input can create a frame trap up to 3f on block (no gap if starting from 6[HK]); puts opponent into limited juggle state; extremely powerful when juggled into (does not increase juggle count at all from DR~6HK)
cells.html: Hitconfirmable ender after 6HK; delaying the input can create a frame trap up to 3f on block (no gap if starting from 6[HK]); puts opponent into limited juggle state; extremely powerful when juggled into (does not increase juggle count at all from DR~6HK)

cells.text: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 2f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
cells.html: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 2f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

cells.text: Side switch; after throwing opponent into corner, can Drive Rush for strong strike/throw mixup; DR~5[HP] trades with reversal 4f normals for a high-damage trade combo; meaty options are much weaker after normal dash (no throw, limited conversions on hit); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
cells.html: Side switch; after throwing opponent into corner, can Drive Rush for strong strike/throw mixup; DR~5[HP] trades with reversal 4f normals for a high-damage trade combo; meaty options are much weaker after normal dash (no throw, limited conversions on hit); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

cells.text: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.
cells.html: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

cells.text: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.
cells.html: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.

cells.text: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.
cells.html: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.

cells.text: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.
cells.html: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

cells.text: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.544 (min/throw), 1.903 (min/block), 3.081 (max/final DR frame)
cells.html: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.544 (min/throw), 1.903 (min/block), 3.081 (max/final DR frame)

cells.text: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties
cells.html: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

cells.text: Cannot hit cross-up; ~65% screen length; Wall Splat on hit; KD Advantage increases when farther from the wall; significant pushback on block makes it hard to punish when spaced out

cells.text: Stance with 1-hit armor can be extended an extra 90f by holding the button, followed by 26 unarmored recovery frames; no armor on lower half of body; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)
cells.html: Stance with 1-hit armor can be extended an extra 90f by holding the button, followed by 26 unarmored recovery frames; no armor on lower half of body; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

cells.text: Stance with 2-hit full-body armor can be extended an extra 30f by holding a kick button, followed by 29 unarmored recovery frames; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

cells.text: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights
cells.html: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

cells.text: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights
cells.html: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

cells.text: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup
cells.html: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

cells.text: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs
cells.html: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs

cells.text: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup
cells.html: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

cells.text: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup
cells.html: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

cells.text: Can hold button 27f for more damage and a cinematic counter that activates against strikes and throws
cells.html: Can hold button 27f for more damage and a cinematic counter that activates against strikes and throws

cells.text: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version
cells.html: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version

cells.text: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing
cells.html: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing

cells.text: Useful anti-air, reversal, and juggle ender; can be steered by holding forward/back; this affects the range, allowing you to hold back for close anti-airs or hold forward in ranged juggles or fireball punishes; upward hit triggers a cinematic; on block/whiff, ends with a downward slam that does 500 less damage than the cinematic version (still does full Drive Damage on hit); Marisa's collision box extends far downward, preventing opponent from walking or dashing underneath (opponent can still jump to avoid 2nd hit)
cells.html: Useful anti-air, reversal, and juggle ender; can be steered by holding forward/back; this affects the range, allowing you to hold back for close anti-airs or hold forward in ranged juggles or fireball punishes; upward hit triggers a cinematic; on block/whiff, ends with a downward slam that does 500 less damage than the cinematic version (still does full Drive Damage on hit); Marisa's collision box extends far downward, preventing opponent from walking or dashing underneath (opponent can still jump to avoid 2nd hit)

cells.text: Takes opponent to corner; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)
cells.html: Takes opponent to corner; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)

cells.text: Takes opponent to corner; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)
cells.html: Takes opponent to corner; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)
